headline: Civil service promotion still 80% seniority-based despite reform pledge
context: Despite the 2024 Public Service Remuneration System overhaul, 80% of promotions in the federal civil service remain seniority-based rather than merit-based.

cards.big: 80% of civil servants promoted by time served, not performance
cards.sub: 2024 reform promised meritocracy but seniority still dominates promotion criteria

cards.big: JPA Circular 4/2024 introduced competency assessment for Grade 48 and above only
cards.sub: 95% of civil servants in Grade 41 and below remain under automatic time-based promotion

cards.big: Malaysia's 1.7 million civil servants cost RM98 billion in emoluments annually
cards.sub: Productivity per employee ranks below Singapore, South Korea, and Thailand benchmarks

cards.big: Top 5 ministries by headcount grew 12% despite public service reform orders
cards.sub: Reform rhetoric has not translated into leaner, more effective government operations

cards.big: Seniority-based promotion rewards patience, not performance — it punishes the best people
Can Malaysia's civil service attract top talent without genuine merit-based advancement?
